prevalence.trend: stable; higher in socially isolated and multimorbid older adults

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Structured assessment with screening tools and differentiation from dementia and medical causes

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: variable; primary care detection pathways exist in many systems
publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: tens of millions of older adults
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate to high with integrated care; varies by member state

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Primary care and geriatric services commonly detect and manage depression
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Psychotherapy and antidepressants with geriatric monitoring; social prescribing and support services
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Service variability and access in rural areas
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong primary care and social support integration in many member states
